summary: review-fleet run on BetterAI: 7 module scanners + 2 cross-cutting + 4 adversarial → organizer into fix waves
logs: Collected 81 raw findings from 9 scanners + 4 adversaries

result.summary: Post-Wave-6 BetterAI MCP server is structurally sound (198 tests pass, 0 typecheck errors) but carries two genuinely critical security holes on the tool surface and a cluster of magic-number / DRY-duplication debt the repo's own rules explicitly forbid. Confirmed by reading source: (1) check_file reads any caller-supplied host path with zero containment check against BETTERAI_PROJECTS_ROOT (arbitrary file / bearer-token exfiltration), and (2) record_memory's hand-rolled serializer never escapes newlines, allowing frontmatter injection that overrides kind/durability on read-back. High-severity items: corpus-supplied ReDoS in check_file, unbounded embedding + repo-detector caches keyed on attacker-controlled text, orphaned McpServer leak when initialize is rejected by the limiter, unbounded POST body, two divergent hand-rolled YAML parsers (CLI vs server) that make `betterai validate` no longer predict server load behavior, CLI validate accepting rules the server silently drops, env contract drift guard that checks types but not default VALUES, and a hardcoded health-probe host. Counts (verified, deduped): 2 critical, 11 high, 17 medium, 10 low. Many medium/low entries are the same no-magic-numbers literals re-reported per scanner and have been merged.

verified_findings.title: check_file reads arbitrary host path with no containment check against BETTERAI_PROJECTS_ROOT
verified_findings.why: `readFileSync(path!, 'utf8')` at line 130 runs on the caller-supplied `path` with zero validation that it resolves inside ctx.config.BETTERAI_PROJECTS_ROOT, despite the file header claiming the path must be inside that root. An authenticated caller (or a localhost-bearer attacker per the threat model) passes path='/data/token' or '/etc/passwd'; the handler reads it, runs corpus regexes against it, and returns each matching line in violations[].evidence (truncated to 240). The scopesQueried/repoRootDetector logic does not gate the read — the read at 127-130 happens unconditionally. Absolute paths are taken as-is so no '..' is even needed.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Before readFileSync: const abs = realpathSync(resolve(path)); assert abs === resolve(projectsRoot) || abs.startsWith(resolve(projectsRoot)+sep), else throw a typed PathOutsideProjectsRoot error. Root is already on ctx.config.BETTERAI_PROJECTS_ROOT.

verified_findings.title: Frontmatter injection: serializeMemory/formatScalar never escapes embedded newlines
verified_findings.why: formatScalar (line 248-256) quotes strings with YAML metacharacters but its escape only handles backslash and double-quote (`.replace(/\\/g,...).replace(/"/g,...)`) — newlines pass through verbatim. An agent-supplied multi-line field (title, project, or any array item such as related_memories) breaks out of the value and injects new column-0 frontmatter keys. Because record_memory serializes arrays after kind/auto_captured and the server reader (reader.ts) is last-write-wins, injected `kind:`/`durability:` lines override the real ones on read-back. The audit event logs the submitted frontmatter, not the corrupted parse, so on-disk corpus diverges from the audit trail. Confirmed against the actual serialize + parse logic.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: In formatScalar, when the value contains \n/\r/control chars, emit a JSON.stringify'd (newline-escaped) quoted scalar; or add .regex(/^[^\n\r]*$/) to title/project and the array-item string schemas in memoryFrontmatterSchema. Cleanest: replace the hand-rolled serializer with the `yaml` package.

verified_findings.title: ReDoS: corpus-supplied regex executed per-line with no timeout or length cap
verified_findings.why: check.pattern is validated only as a compilable RegExp (line 166), then `new RegExp(check.pattern)` is run via lineRe.test(line) on every line (177-180). The per-call CorpusReader loads `<repo-root>/.betterai` rules (138-145), so an attacker who controls a checked-out repo controls the pattern. A catastrophic-backtracking pattern like `(a+)+$` against a long line (inline_contents is unbounded) blocks Node's single event loop, stalling all sessions. The connection limiter caps concurrency but does nothing for one hung synchronous call.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Run matching under a hard timeout (worker) or use a linear-time engine (re2); at minimum cap line length and reject/skip patterns that exceed a budget. Optionally pre-screen patterns with safe-regex at corpus load.

verified_findings.title: Unbounded embedding cache keyed on attacker-controlled query text
verified_findings.why: EmbeddingScorer.cache is a plain `Map<string,number[]>` (line 207) with no max size, no TTL, never cleared. The scorer is a process singleton shared across all sessions. similarities() caches the per-query embedding under contentHash(queryText(ctx)) where queryText = `${ctx.intent}\n${ctx.file_paths.join(' ')}` — fully caller-controlled (lines 248-249, 262-265, 273). Each distinct intent inserts a ~384-float vector pinned for process lifetime; varying intent also busts the response LRU so misses always reach the scorer. A client issuing many distinct intents OOMs the container.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Back the cache with lru-cache capped by entry count (mirror ContextCache 256/60s), or never persist the per-query vector (it embeds once per call anyway) and cache only corpus-artifact vectors. Lift the cap to a named constant.

verified_findings.title: Orphaned McpServer/transport leak when initialize is rejected by the limiter
verified_findings.why: For a new session the handler calls createMcpServer(), constructs the transport, and `await server.connect(transport)` (187-202) BEFORE dispatchWithLimiter (206). When the limiter queue is full, limiter.acquire() throws TooManyInFlightError synchronously so transport.handleRequest never runs, onsessioninitialized (190) never fires, and the session is never inserted into the sessions map. gcIdleSessions and closeAllSessions only iterate the map, so the connected McpServer + transport (all 7 tools registered) are unreachable and never closed. Every 429'd initialize under burst permanently leaks one server until process restart.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Acquire the limiter permit BEFORE constructing/connecting the transport, or wrap the new-session path so a TooManyInFlightError (or any handleRequest throw) triggers transport.close()+server.close() in catch/finally before returning 429.

verified_findings.title: POST /mcp parses request body with no size or nesting limit
verified_findings.why: `await c.req.json()` (line 173) buffers and JSON.parses the entire request body before any zod schema runs; no hono bodyLimit middleware is registered and no Content-Length cap exists. A multi-hundred-MB body OOMs the shared process; large intent/recent_diff strings (no .max() in retrieve-context.ts:33-40) then flow into the orchestrator and cache key. One large body degrades every session.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Register hono bodyLimit() on /mcp with a named MAX_REQUEST_BYTES; add z.string().max(N) to intent/recent_diff and z.array().max(K) to file_paths/symbols.

verified_findings.title: RepoDetector cache grows unbounded across distinct caller-supplied paths
verified_findings.why: The cache Map (line 55) is keyed by resolve(hostPath) and only ever deleted when the SAME key is re-queried and found stale (line 78) — no size cap, no LRU, no periodic GC. detectFromBatch(file_paths) is on the retrieve_context hot path with fully caller-controlled file_paths[0]. N retrieve_context calls each with a unique path insert N permanent entries; memory grows linearly until OOM. The 64-iteration walk cap bounds per-call CPU but not cache size.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Bound the cache with an LRU (max-entries named constant) or opportunistically sweep expired entries on each call the way http-sse.ts gcIdleSessions does.

verified_findings.title: Two independent hand-rolled YAML frontmatter parsers that have already diverged
verified_findings.why: frontmatter.ts (CLI gate: validate/why/replay) and reader.ts (server load path) parse the same corpus frontmatter subset with separate implementations that disagree on observable behavior: (1) `>` block scalar is folded with spaces in CLI (line 118) but joined with \n in server (reader.ts:280); (2) trailing-newline trim differs; (3) malformed/non-conforming top-level keys throw in CLI (68-69) but are silently skipped in server (reader.ts:216-219); (4) empty-value key becomes null in CLI vs unset in server. A file can pass `betterai validate` yet be dropped or mis-shaped by the server. Only reader.ts is tested.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Extract one shared parser module imported by both reader.ts and the CLI; reconcile the >-fold / empty-string / throw-vs-skip semantics deliberately in the one place. Keep the ParseOk/ParseErr line-reporting wrapper in the CLI layer.

verified_findings.title: CLI validate accepts rules the server silently drops (no check.kind / array-shape / type validation)
verified_findings.why: validateFile only checks presence of required fields plus category/severity/kind/durability enums (159-205). It never validates check.kind (must be regex|ast-grep), applies_when.paths/intents being arrays, steps_count numeric, context_keywords array, or auto_captured boolean. The server's Zod schemas in reader.ts enforce all of these and DROP the artifact with a ValidationIssue on failure. So `betterai validate` exits 0 on a rule the server will silently never load — the exact 'validates in CLI but loads broken' failure mode.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Share the server Zod schemas (or a contracts module) and run safeParse in validateFile instead of the bespoke required-field loop, so CLI validation equals the server load predicate.

verified_findings.title: CLI frontmatter parser (src/cli/_shared/frontmatter.ts) has zero direct test coverage
verified_findings.why: No test imports parseFrontmatter or _shared/frontmatter.ts (verified by grep across src/__tests__). frontmatter-robustness.test.ts exercises only the SERVER reader.ts. Since validate/why/replay route through this untested parser, a parser regression (e.g. the nested-list class of bug Wave-3 fixed in the server) ships undetected on the CLI side and the CLI/server divergence above is unpinned.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Add cli-frontmatter.test.ts mirroring server G12 cases (nested block lists, inline flow arrays, empty [] arrays, multi-line | and > scalars) plus a divergence guard that parses the same fixtures through both parsers.

verified_findings.title: Env drift guard checks types only, not default VALUES — defaults can silently diverge
verified_findings.why: EnvContractDriftChecks uses MutuallyAssignable (line 160), which compares only Zod-inferred TYPES; z.string().default('/data') and default('/other') both infer to string. The live EnvSchema in main.ts:104-128 re-declares every default as a hardcoded literal instead of importing the DEFAULT_* constants, and the runtime drift test only parses the CONTRACT schema, never the live main.ts schema. Editing a default in one file and not the other passes typecheck and every guard while producing different ports/paths at runtime — exactly the divergence this module exists to prevent.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Make main.ts import EnvSchema (or the DEFAULT_* constants) from contracts/env.ts and delete the inline duplicate, collapsing the two schemas. Until then, add a runtime test parsing the LIVE main.ts EnvSchema with {} asserting each default equals the contract DEFAULT_*.

verified_findings.title: main.ts re-declares the whole env schema inline, re-hardcoding every host/port/path the env layer owns
verified_findings.why: contracts/env.ts is documented as the ONLY place hosts/ports/paths are hardcoded and exports DEFAULT_CORPUS_ROOT, DEFAULT_AUDIT_PATH, DEFAULT_PROJECTS_ROOT, DEFAULT_MCP_PORT=7777, DEFAULT_TOKEN_PATH, DEFAULT_BIND_HOST='127.0.0.1'. main.ts:104-128 duplicates all of them as bare literals. A change to any default must be made twice or the live server and the contract drift; the drift guard only checks type shape (see env.ts finding), so values diverge undetected.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Import EnvSchema and DEFAULT_* from ../contracts/env.js, delete the inline duplicate, and invert the ResolvedEnv import direction so main.ts consumes the contract.

verified_findings.title: Health-probe URL hardcodes host '127.0.0.1' instead of the env layer's DEFAULT_BIND_HOST
verified_findings.why: `http://127.0.0.1:${port}/health` reads the port from BETTERAI_MCP_PORT/DEFAULT_MCP_PORT but pins the host as a raw literal — the exact '127.0.0.1' string config-from-env-not-hardcoded names as its anti-pattern. If the server binds a non-loopback BETTERAI_BIND_HOST, the gate's reachability check probes the wrong host and reports a spurious server-down warning. The env layer already exposes DEFAULT_BIND_HOST/BETTERAI_BIND_HOST.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Read process.env.BETTERAI_BIND_HOST ?? DEFAULT_BIND_HOST (imported from ../contracts/env.js) into the health URL instead of the literal.

verified_findings.title: No test exercises the check_file handler — path traversal, inline_contents XOR, or ReDoS
verified_findings.why: check_file appears in tests only in the mcp-e2e tools/list count assertion (line 183) and the import; checkFile.handler is never invoked. No test passes a traversal path, asserts the path/inline_contents XOR error, exercises an invalid or catastrophic regex, or verifies the single 'check' audit event. The critical path-traversal and high ReDoS defects ship completely unguarded.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Add check-file.test.ts: (a) both/neither of path|inline_contents -> VALIDATION_ERROR, (b) path outside projects root -> rejection (after fix), (c) inline_contents matching rule asserts line numbers + truncation, (d) invalid regex lands in skipped_checks, (e) exactly one 'check' audit event.

verified_findings.title: LRU default max (256) and TTL (60_000) hardcoded inline — the exact anti-pattern the repo rule cites by file:line
verified_findings.why: `max: opts.max ?? 256` / `ttl: opts.ttlMs ?? 60_000`. no-magic-numbers-import-from-constants.md quotes context-hash.ts:78-79 verbatim as its canonical anti-pattern and prescribes src/server/cache/constants.ts exporting LRU_DEFAULT_MAX=256 and LRU_DEFAULT_TTL_MS=60_000. These policy values are duplicated in cache/index.ts and main.ts.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Create src/server/cache/constants.ts with LRU_DEFAULT_MAX=256 and LRU_DEFAULT_TTL_MS=60_000 (with the rule's comments) and import here.

verified_findings.title: createContextCache duplicates the 256 / 60_000 LRU defaults a third time
verified_findings.why: `max: opts.max ?? 256` / `ttl: opts.ttlMs ?? 60_000` — byte-identical to context-hash.ts:78-79. The 'Defaults match the retrieval cache' comment (line 39) is a manual cross-reference the type system does not enforce; if one site changes, the two caches in the same path use different staleness budgets.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Import LRU_DEFAULT_MAX / LRU_DEFAULT_TTL_MS from src/server/cache/constants.ts.

verified_findings.title: Concurrency cap (16) and queue cap (64) hardcoded as inline defaults
verified_findings.why: `this.maxInFlight = opts.maxInFlight ?? 16; this.queueMax = opts.queueMax ?? 64;` are the backpressure thresholds that decide when the server returns 429, sourced from 'eng review §1.5' only in a comment and restated in main.ts:191. No named export to grep when tuning fan-out limits.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Export LIMITER_DEFAULT_MAX_IN_FLIGHT=16 and LIMITER_DEFAULT_QUEUE_MAX=64 from src/server/cache/constants.ts and reference in the constructor fallback.

verified_findings.title: Cache + limiter capacity/TTL defaults hardcoded at the wiring site (256 / 60_000 / 16 / 64)
verified_findings.why: `new ContextCache({ max: 256, ttlMs: 60_000 })` and `new ConnectionLimiter({ maxInFlight: 16, queueMax: 64 })` bake the same four policy literals that already exist as class-default fallbacks, so each value is now defined in two places and can drift.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Import LRU_DEFAULT_MAX / LRU_DEFAULT_TTL_MS / LIMITER_DEFAULT_MAX_IN_FLIGHT / LIMITER_DEFAULT_QUEUE_MAX from src/server/cache/constants.ts (or construct with no options to let class defaults apply).

verified_findings.title: Default router config (domains, max_rules_per_domain 4, max_total_rules 12) duplicated between emptyConfig() and normalize()
verified_findings.why: The triple {domains:['maintainability','methodology'], max_rules_per_domain:4, max_total_rules:12} is written in emptyConfig() (69-71) and again as ?? fallbacks in normalize() (217-219). The 'no config file' path and the 'config present, key absent' path can silently diverge, producing different routing budgets. No single source of truth.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Extract DEFAULT_ROUTER_DEFAULTS (DEFAULT_MAX_RULES_PER_DOMAIN=4, DEFAULT_MAX_TOTAL_RULES=12, DEFAULT_DOMAINS) at the top of router.ts and reference from both functions.

verified_findings.title: Grep scoring weights (severity 3/2/1, component points 3/2/2/1) are bare literals scattered through the scorers
verified_findings.why: SEVERITY_WEIGHT={high:3,medium:2,low:1} plus raw += 3 (path), += 2 (symbol/intent), += 1 (body) across scoreRule/scoreSkill/scoreMemory. These are the core ranking policy; embeddings.ts documents 'a path-glob hit is worth raw 3 x severity weight' and the hybrid blend was tuned against these magnitudes, yet they live as inline literals. Changing the relative weight means hunting bare 2s and 3s.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Add named SCORE_PATH_MATCH=3, SCORE_SYMBOL_MATCH=2, SCORE_INTENT_MATCH=2, SCORE_BODY_HIT=1 (and a decision bonus) at the top of grep.ts and reference them in all three scorers.

verified_findings.title: Skill/memory top-k floor (4) and divisor (3) are bare literals in orchestration logic
verified_findings.why: `Math.max(4, Math.floor(route.max_total_rules / 3))` decides how many skills and memories each retrieval returns — the minimum surfaced items and the rules:kind ratio — as unnamed literals at the call site. A reviewer cannot tell they are tunable or whether the 4 here shares intent with the router's max_rules_per_domain 4.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Lift to MIN_TOPK_PER_KIND=4 and RULES_TO_KIND_DIVISOR=3 (or a defaultKindTopK helper) and import them.

verified_findings.title: RepoDetector cache TTL-expiry, mtime-invalidation, and 64-iter cap paths are untested
verified_findings.why: Tests construct RepoDetector only with stopAt (orchestrator.test.ts:232, retrieve-context.test.ts:161) and never inject now/ttlMs, and scope-detection.test.ts goes through detect.ts which builds a fresh detector per call (no cache). The class's distinguishing logic — TTL freshness (73), mtime-mismatch invalidation (74-78), stopAt short-circuit (116), 64-iteration cap (105) — has no test. A regression in the invalidation predicate (cache forever / never cache) passes the suite.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Add repo-detector.test.ts with now/ttlMs injection: (a) repeated detect within TTL returns cached, (b) advancing now past ttlMs re-walks, (c) touching .git/HEAD mtime invalidates within TTL, (d) a symlink loop terminates at the 64-iter cap with repo_root null.

verified_findings.title: Waiter.reject is captured but never invoked — queued callers have no rejection/timeout path
verified_findings.why: The Waiter interface declares reject (27) and acquire() captures it (72), but release() only ever resolve()s the next waiter (80); nothing calls a queued waiter's reject. If a permit-holder's fn never settles, every queued waiter's promise (and its awaiting HTTP handler) hangs indefinitely with no abort, while the dead reject field implies a cancellation path that does not exist.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Either remove the reject field to make the no-cancellation design explicit, or wire an acquire timeout / shutdown drain that calls waiter.reject(new TooManyInFlightError(...)) and removes it from the queue.

verified_findings.title: Unauthenticated /health bypass forces a synchronous audit-log disk write on every request
verified_findings.why: The /health bypass branch (139-151) runs onBypass BEFORE the host-allowlist check (154) and requires no token; main.ts wires onBypass to a synchronous appendFileSync audit append. The connection limiter only wraps POST /mcp dispatch, not /health. An unauthenticated loopback caller looping curl on /health drives unbounded audit-log growth and saturates synchronous disk I/O on the request thread — an unauthenticated I/O DoS plus audit-log flooding that buries real security events.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Throttle/sample the health-bypass audit emission (coalesce to at most one event per N seconds per source) and/or rate-limit /health; do not synchronously append on an unauthenticated unthrottled path.

verified_findings.title: Block-scalar fold marker '>' is treated identically to literal '|' (newlines not folded)
verified_findings.why: For any non-empty marker, mode is set to 'block' (232-233) and collected lines are always joined with '\n' at 280 — identical for '|' and '>'. Per YAML, '>' must fold internal newlines to spaces. A '>'-folded prose field (e.g. when_to_use, fix_template) gets embedded raw newlines instead of a single folded line, differing from what the author wrote. The CLI parser folds '>' correctly (frontmatter.ts:118), so the two parsers produce different output for the same file.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Track the marker when entering block mode; at emit, join with ' ' (trimmed) for '>' and '\n' for '|', mirroring frontmatter.ts consumeMultilineString.

verified_findings.title: Inline flow array split on commas corrupts quoted items containing commas
verified_findings.why: parseScalar splits flow sequences on every comma (inner.split(',')) with no quote awareness. `intents: ["review, then merge", "plan"]` splits into three garbage items. Zod still sees a string[] so it passes and the wrong values flow into retrieval matching — a silent data-corruption path with no validation guard. frontmatter.ts shares the identical limitation.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Parse the flow array respecting quoted segments, or detect a comma inside a quoted item and emit a ValidationIssue rather than mis-splitting.

verified_findings.title: Duplicate ids WITHIN a single scope are silently kept (no dedupe, no issue)
verified_findings.why: mergeByIdOverride only de-duplicates across scopes (repo replaces global). Two files in the SAME root with the same id both survive; findRule/.find() returns the first by directory-walk order (non-deterministic across filesystems) and retrieval can surface the id twice. No ValidationIssue is recorded for the collision.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Detect repeated ids within a scope when building each scope's list (or in mergeByIdOverride) and drop the later one with a ValidationIssue (or fail loud); ensure deterministic ordering.

verified_findings.title: Inline one-off Error subclasses instead of a central errors layer
verified_findings.why: check-file.ts (253-259), record-memory.ts (197-203), and explain-rule.ts (160-166) each define their own Error subclass with ad-hoc code strings. typed-errors-from-errors-layer.md requires error classes from a central src/errors/ layer extending BetterAIError with stable BAI-* codes and factories; its applies_when lists exactly these intents (validation, io, fs) and the src/**/*.ts glob. No src/errors/ directory exists. Clients cannot match on a stable code table.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Create src/errors/ with BetterAIError base + factories (Errors.validation, Errors.ruleNotFound, Errors.pathOutsideProjectsRoot) carrying BAI-* codes; throw via factory at the three sites.

verified_findings.title: closeAllSessions resolves before sessions actually close and swallows close errors
verified_findings.why: closeAllSessions is async but awaits nothing: destroySession (107-108) fires `void transport.close().catch(()=>{})` and `void server.close().catch(()=>{})`. main.ts shutdown awaits closeAllSessions then immediately closes the listener, believing sessions are drained, but the awaited promise resolves synchronously while every close is still pending, and close errors are silently dropped. Graceful shutdown does not actually drain; in-flight SSE writes can error against a closing listener.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Have destroySession return the close promises and have closeAllSessions await Promise.allSettled over them so shutdown blocks until every close completes; log rather than swallow rejections.

verified_findings.title: Internal error message leaked to client in 500 response body
verified_findings.why: On a non-limiter dispatch failure the 500 path returns `detail: (err as Error).message` (225). Downstream errors carry internals — AuditIoError messages embed absolute filesystem paths (jsonl.ts) and other layers surface config/path detail. Echoing raw internal messages to the caller is information disclosure.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Return a generic { error: 'internal_error' } to the client and log the full detail server-side.

verified_findings.title: allowedHostsFromEnv returns an empty Set for a comma/whitespace-only BETTERAI_ALLOWED_HOSTS, bricking auth
verified_findings.why: The guard is `if (env.BETTERAI_ALLOWED_HOSTS)`. A value like ',' or ' , ' is truthy, enters the branch, then split/trim/filter(length>0) removes every entry, returning an empty Set. The bearer middleware does `if (!allowedHosts.has(host)) return 401` (bearer.ts:154), so an empty allowlist rejects every request including loopback. A misconfigured var silently disables the server with no fallback to the derived default and no error.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: After split/trim/filter, if the array is empty, throw a typed config error or fall through to the derived BIND_HOST:PORT default (treat 'present but parses to nothing' like 'absent').

verified_findings.title: Hand-rolled router YAML parser (parseYaml/loadRouterYaml/fromFile) has zero test coverage
verified_findings.why: Every test constructs DomainRouter with an inline config object; no test calls DomainRouter.fromFile, loadRouterYaml, or parseYaml (verified by grep). The ~120-line parser (block lists, inline lists, nested maps, scalar coercion, the `as never` edge at 109) is the production load path for _meta/domain-router.yaml yet no branch is exercised. Parser regressions degrade routing to defaults or drop config silently.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Add a router-parser unit test feeding the real domain-router.yaml plus edge shapes (empty value + sibling, inline list, nested router objects) through parseYaml/normalize and asserting the resulting config.

verified_findings.title: No test for record_memory write path, INSERT-IGNORE collision, scope=repo validation, or injection round-trip
verified_findings.why: No test references record_memory's handler, serializeMemory, or formatScalar (verified by grep). The id-collision already_existed branch (140-146), scope='repo' with no repo root ValidationError (109-117), the 0o640 mode, the kebab-case id guard, and the newline-injection break-out (critical finding above) are all untested. A round-trip test with a newline-bearing title/related_memories would fail today.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Add record-memory.test.ts: write-then-reread round trip asserting persisted frontmatter equals submitted (no forged/overridden keys); second write same id returns already_existed without overwrite; scope='repo' with empty context throws VALIDATION_ERROR; non-kebab id rejected.

verified_findings.title: No test exercises the idle-session GC reaping path in http-sse.ts
verified_findings.why: registerHttpSseTransport accepts idleSessionGcMs and a now() injection (95-96) specifically to test reaping, but no test drives gcIdleSessions to evict a session and assert sessionCount drops / destroySession runs (verified by grep for idleSessionGcMs/gcIdle/lastSeen/reaped). The GC lifecycle — the core statefulness contract — is untested, so an off-by-one in the >idleGcMs comparison (113) or the SSE-reap behavior ships undetected.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Add an http-sse unit test with a tiny idleSessionGcMs and controllable now(): create a session, advance the clock past the window, fire another request to trigger the sweep, assert sessionCount()===0 and transport.close was called.

verified_findings.title: Limiter-to-transport 429 integration (dispatchWithLimiter) is untested
verified_findings.why: ConnectionLimiter is unit-tested in isolation but its wiring into POST /mcp via dispatchWithLimiter (211-229) is not: the TooManyInFlightError->429 body { error:'too_many_in_flight', retry_after_ms:250 } translation, and that GET/DELETE deliberately bypass the limiter (deadlock-avoidance contract). A regression that wraps GET in the limiter or changes the 429 shape would pass CI.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Add a test injecting a limiter that always throws TooManyInFlightError, POST /mcp asserts 429 with the documented body; plus a test that a GET /mcp on a live session is not gated by the limiter.

verified_findings.title: gate --status 'day N' uses elapsed-24h periods but labels and active-days metric use calendar dates
verified_findings.why: dayN = floor((nowMs-startedMs)/MS_PER_DAY)+1 counts elapsed 24h periods, but the comment claims calendar semantics ('the day containing started_at is day 1') and activeDays is keyed on calendar date strings (ts.slice(0,10), line 231). A gate started at 23:00 reports 'Day 1/5' two hours later while activeDays already holds two dates; the pro-rata need() projection mixes the two notions. Near day boundaries this misreports ON TRACK/BEHIND and can flip the exit code.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Compute dayN from calendar dates (matching activeDays) or restate everything in elapsed-24h terms; pick one and make dayN, the comment, and activeDays consistent.

verified_findings.title: runWeeklyGate re-hardcodes gate targets (5/5/3) and window math instead of reusing the named constants
verified_findings.why: The file defines GATE_TARGET_DAYS=5, GATE_TARGET_SESSIONS=5, GATE_TARGET_BEHAVIOR_CHANGES=3, MS_PER_DAY, used by the start/status paths. runWeeklyGate independently hardcodes the same values inline: '7*24*60*60*1000' (353), 'consecutive>=5' (387), 'distinctRules.size>=5' (393), 'behaviorChanges>=3' (399). A future threshold tweak changes start/status but not the week verb, producing two definitions of 'pass'.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Reuse GATE_TARGET_* and MS_PER_DAY (add WEEK_WINDOW_DAYS=7) in runWeeklyGate instead of the inline literals.

verified_findings.title: CLI walkers descend into _meta/ where the server reader excludes it, causing false validation errors
verified_findings.why: validate.ts walkMarkdown skips only files starting with '_' but recurses into every directory, so it reads rules/_meta/schema.md etc. (no frontmatter) and reports 'frontmatter parse failed', exiting 1. The server reader.ts walkMarkdown explicitly skips directories named '_meta' (line 336). The same blindness exists in status.ts/why.ts/replay.ts walkers (inflates counts). Latent today because active corpus roots ship no _meta dir, but any corpus with the documented _meta convention breaks CLI validate while loading fine on the server.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Match the server: skip directories named '_meta' in the CLI walkMarkdown generators (validate.ts, status.ts, why.ts, replay.ts), not just '_'-prefixed files.

verified_findings.title: Two repo-root walk-up implementations encode the same '.git ascent' contract and diverge
verified_findings.why: detectRepoRoot (CLI) and RepoDetector.walkUp (server) both implement 'resolve start, ascend up to 64 parents, stop at the dir with .git'. They differ: (a) CLI statSyncs .git and accepts dir-or-file (worktree support) while the server uses bare existsSync; (b) the server stops at HOME and '/' (stopAt) but the CLI walks to '/'. They can resolve a DIFFERENT repo root for the same path (a repo under HOME, or a worktree), silently changing scope (repo vs global) between `betterai new` and the server's record-memory/retrieve.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Have cli/_shared/repo-root.ts delegate to the server's RepoDetector/detectRepoRoot, or extract one shared walk-up helper both import, so worktree handling and stop boundaries are defined once.

verified_findings.title: Repo-detector cache TTL default (60_000) is a bare literal
verified_findings.why: `this.ttlMs = opts.ttlMs ?? 60_000;` hardcodes the 60s repo-detection cache TTL (the branch-switch staleness budget documented in the file header) as a literal — exactly the TTL the no-magic-numbers rule says must be a named constant.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Lift to a named REPO_DETECT_CACHE_TTL_MS=60_000 constant in repo-detector.ts and use it as the default.

verified_findings.title: Ranking weights in finalScore() are bare literals (severity 1.0/0.66/0.33/0.5, kind 1.0/0.8/0.6)
verified_findings.why: sev = high?1.0:medium?0.66:low?0.33:0.5 and kindWeight = rule?1.0:skill?0.8:memory?0.6 are the 'why/what would fire' simulation weights, meant to mirror the server's grep.ts ranking but tied to it by nothing — the CLI preview can silently diverge from what the server returns, defeating the tool's purpose.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Lift to named SEVERITY_WEIGHTS/KIND_WEIGHTS and ideally share with the server scorer so preview and live ranking cannot drift.

verified_findings.title: Scope-resolution + target-root construction duplicated between `new` CLI and record-memory
verified_findings.why: Both implement the same scope decision ('repo if repoRoot detected else global; explicit repo with no root is an error') and target-root rule ('repo => join(repoRoot,".betterai"); global => corpus root'). They derive repoRoot from different inputs (CLI from cwd via the CLI walk-up; record-memory from context.file_paths via server detectRepoRoot) and via the two divergent walk-up parsers, so the same logical decision can compute differently.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Extract a shared resolveScopeAndRoot({requestedScope, repoRoot, globalRoot}) helper returning {scope, rootDir} and call it from both sites.

verified_findings.title: Concurrent retrievals duplicate embed work on the shared singleton cache (no in-flight de-dup)
verified_findings.why: Two concurrent similarities() calls with the same intent both compute their missing set before awaiting embedder(missing) (256-271), so the same query/artifact text is embedded twice. Correctness holds (idempotent writes) but the model is invoked redundantly on the hot path under concurrent load — an efficiency smell on a shared mutable cache, not a crash.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Store in-flight embedding promises keyed by content hash so concurrent callers await the same embed, or document that similarities is called sequentially per process.

verified_findings.title: chmod-to-0o640 is skipped when an external actor recreates the audit file between existsSync and appendFileSync
verified_findings.why: createsFile is computed from existsSync (224) then appendFileSync runs (225); if an external rotator removes the file in that TOCTOU window, appendFileSync creates a NEW file but createsFile is false so the explicit chmodSync(AUDIT_FILE_MODE) at 229 is skipped. The new file's mode is then only the umask-masked default — the deterministic 0o640 contract is not guaranteed on that raced path. Low because under a typical umask the masked mode is still 0o640.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Always chmodSync(this.path, AUDIT_FILE_MODE) after a successful append (idempotent) instead of gating it on the pre-append existsSync result.

verified_findings.title: Auth-bypass audit record trusts attacker-controlled X-Forwarded-For / X-Real-IP with no proxy in front
verified_findings.why: onBypass info.ip is taken from c.req.header('x-forwarded-for') ?? 'x-real-ip' ?? 'unknown' (143-146). main.ts serves the hono app directly via @hono/node-server with no reverse proxy and no trust-proxy gate, so these headers are client-supplied. `curl -H 'X-Forwarded-For: 8.8.8.8' .../health` writes an audit event attributing the only unauthenticated endpoint's access to a forged value, defeating the bypass-attribution intent of mcp-tools-require-bearer.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Use the real socket remote address for the audit ip; only honor X-Forwarded-For when an explicit trusted-proxy flag is set in the env layer.

verified_findings.title: No cap on concurrent MCP sessions; initialize spam grows the session map
verified_findings.why: sessions.set (191) on every successful initialize has no max-session guard; GET/DELETE and the limiter only gate POST dispatch, not session creation. A client sending many initialize POSTs without DELETE creates many McpServer+transport pairs, reclaimed only by the 30-minute idle GC, so a burst within the window holds thousands of servers. Lower severity than the orphan leak because it requires a valid bearer and the GC eventually reaps.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Add a maxSessions cap (named constant); on exceed, evict the oldest idle session or reject initialize with a structured 429 before constructing the McpServer.

verified_findings.title: Evidence-truncation width 240 is a bare literal
verified_findings.why: `truncate(line, 240)` hardcodes the evidence-snippet cap (a payload-size policy returned to clients) inline. A sibling tool truncating evidence would re-pick the number — the drift the no-magic-numbers rule targets.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Extract CHECK_EVIDENCE_MAX_CHARS=240 and reference it.

verified_findings.title: top_k caps/defaults are bare literals duplicated across the four retrieve tools
verified_findings.why: retrieve-context .max(32).default(4); retrieve-rules .max(64).default(8); retrieve-skills/retrieve-memories .max(64).default(4). Result-budget policy duplicated as literals across four files with no shared constant, so they drift (note 32 vs 64) and a reviewer cannot tell whether the divergence is intentional.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Define shared TOP_K_*_DEFAULT/MAX constants in one module and reference from each tool's zod schema.

verified_findings.title: Memory title length cap (80) is a bare literal in the schema
verified_findings.why: `title: z.string().min(1).max(80)` expresses a corpus content policy as a bare literal that can drift from corpus-authoring docs.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Extract MEMORY_TITLE_MAX_CHARS=80 (ideally in a corpus-schema constants module shared with validators).

verified_findings.title: Default lookback window (7 days) and stale-rule horizon (30d) are bare/inline; MS_PER_DAY re-spelled
verified_findings.why: `const cutoff = Date.now() - sinceDays*24*60*60*1000` with sinceDays defaulting to 7, and the file header documents a 30d stale horizon. The digest window and stale threshold are reporting-policy literals not lifted to constants; MS_PER_DAY is re-spelled inline here, in gate.ts and why.ts rather than shared.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Extract DEFAULT_DIGEST_DAYS=7, STALE_RULE_DAYS=30, and a shared MS_PER_DAY used across replay.ts/gate.ts/why.ts.

verified_findings.title: Walk-up depth cap (64) hardcoded; duplicated in cli/_shared/repo-root.ts
verified_findings.why: `for (let i = 0; i < 64; i += 1)` caps directory ascent (symlink-loop protection). The identical 64 is independently restated in cli/_shared/repo-root.ts, so the server detector and CLI helper can disagree on max depth.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Define MAX_WALK_UP_DEPTH=64 in one shared module and import in both.

verified_findings.title: AuditEventSchema is non-strict, so the runtime drift test cannot catch a live field the contract forgot to mirror
verified_findings.why: AuditEventSchema is a plain z.object (no .strict()); Zod strips unknown keys on parse. The static type-level guard (MutuallyAssignable) still catches a missing field at typecheck, but the runtime smoke test (contracts-drift.test.ts) round-trips a fixture through .parse and would silently accept/drop extra fields — so the runtime guard contributes nothing to field-set drift detection despite the module comment implying it adds a layer.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Use z.object({...}).strict() so .parse() rejects rather than strips undeclared keys, making the runtime smoke test a real drift check.

verified_findings.title: `(err as Error).message` assumes the thrown value is an Error
verified_findings.why: The 500 catch casts an unknown caught value to Error and reads .message; a non-Error rejection (thrown string/undefined) yields `detail: undefined`, masking the real failure shape.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Use `const detail = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)` — and combine with the disclosure fix so it is not surfaced to the client at all.

verified_findings.title: parseBlock dedent-before-child case returns a malformed tuple cast `as never`, truncating a mapping
verified_findings.why: Line 109 returns `[emptyOrFirst()] as never` — a length-1 array, not the declared [node,index] tuple. When the map branch destructures it (~170), `next` becomes undefined, so i=undefined, the enclosing while (i<lines.length) is false, and remaining keys are silently dropped. Fires for a mapping key with an empty value followed by a same-indent sibling (a legal shape the parser claims to support). The current domain-router.yaml does not hit it, but a hand-edited config could lose routers/defaults with no error; the `as never` suppresses the type check.
verified_findings.fix_sketch: Return a well-formed [{}, j] from the dedent-before-child case and drop the `as never` so the tuple type is enforced.

fix_waves.title: Introduce shared cache constants module (foundation for cache magic-number fixes)
finding_titles: LRU default max (256) and TTL (60_000) hardcoded inline — the exact anti-pattern the repo rule cites by file:line

fix_waves.title: Consume cache constants + independent single-file magic-number/DRY extractions (non-overlapping files)
finding_titles: LRU default max (256) and TTL (60_000) hardcoded inline — the exact anti-pattern the repo rule cites by file:line
createContextCache duplicates the 256 / 60_000 LRU defaults a third time
Concurrency cap (16) and queue cap (64) hardcoded as inline defaults
Default router config (domains, max_rules_per_domain 4, max_total_rules 12) duplicated between emptyConfig() and normalize()
Skill/memory top-k floor (4) and divisor (3) are bare literals in orchestration logic
runWeeklyGate re-hardcodes gate targets (5/5/3) and window math instead of reusing the named constants
CLI walkers descend into _meta/ where the server reader excludes it, causing false validation errors
Evidence-truncation width 240 is a bare literal
AuditEventSchema is non-strict, so the runtime drift test cannot catch a live field the contract forgot to mirror

report_only: check_file reads arbitrary host path with no containment check against BETTERAI_PROJECTS_ROOT

ReDoS: corpus-supplied regex executed per-line with no timeout or length cap
Unbounded embedding cache keyed on attacker-controlled query text
Orphaned McpServer/transport leak when initialize is rejected by the limiter
POST /mcp parses request body with no size or nesting limit
RepoDetector cache grows unbounded across distinct caller-supplied paths
Two independent hand-rolled YAML frontmatter parsers that have already diverged
CLI validate accepts rules the server silently drops (no check.kind / array-shape / type validation)
CLI frontmatter parser (src/cli/_shared/frontmatter.ts) has zero direct test coverage
Env drift guard checks types only, not default VALUES — defaults can silently diverge
main.ts re-declares the whole env schema inline, re-hardcoding every host/port/path the env layer owns
Health-probe URL hardcodes host '127.0.0.1' instead of the env layer's DEFAULT_BIND_HOST
No test exercises the check_file handler — path traversal, inline_contents XOR, or ReDoS
Grep scoring weights (severity 3/2/1, component points 3/2/2/1) are bare literals scattered through the scorers
RepoDetector cache TTL-expiry, mtime-invalidation, and 64-iter cap paths are untested
Waiter.reject is captured but never invoked — queued callers have no rejection/timeout path
Unauthenticated /health bypass forces a synchronous audit-log disk write on every request
Block-scalar fold marker '>' is treated identically to literal '|' (newlines not folded)
Inline flow array split on commas corrupts quoted items containing commas
Duplicate ids WITHIN a single scope are silently kept (no dedupe, no issue)
Inline one-off Error subclasses instead of a central errors layer
closeAllSessions resolves before sessions actually close and swallows close errors
Internal error message leaked to client in 500 response body
allowedHostsFromEnv returns an empty Set for a comma/whitespace-only BETTERAI_ALLOWED_HOSTS, bricking auth
Hand-rolled router YAML parser (parseYaml/loadRouterYaml/fromFile) has zero test coverage
No test for record_memory write path, INSERT-IGNORE collision, scope=repo validation, or injection round-trip
No test exercises the idle-session GC reaping path in http-sse.ts
Limiter-to-transport 429 integration (dispatchWithLimiter) is untested
gate --status 'day N' uses elapsed-24h periods but labels and active-days metric use calendar dates

Ranking weights in finalScore() are bare literals (severity 1.0/0.66/0.33/0.5, kind 1.0/0.8/0.6)
Scope-resolution + target-root construction duplicated between `new` CLI and record-memory
Concurrent retrievals duplicate embed work on the shared singleton cache (no in-flight de-dup)
